manifest.name: Agent Console

name: Agent Console

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Agent Console v1.2.0 – the first feature release since 1.0, with a bulletproof first run and a cleaner command palette.

## What's new

- **Your first chat just works** – on a fresh install, Agent Console now detects an agent you already have installed, opens the panel for you once, and shows a getting-started state so you're never staring at a dead end.
- **One clear way to start a chat** – the tangle of start-a-chat commands is gone. Use **New chat**, or **New chat with agent…** to pick an agent from a list. Both open a fresh tab; to clear and restart the current tab, use **Hard reload session**.
- **Bring your setup with you** – switching from the Agent Client plugin? Import its agent definitions, defaults, and API keys in one click, offered automatically on first run.
- **See every session at a glance** – the tab-list dropdown now prefixes each tab with its status (ready, busy, needs permission, error, disconnected), so you can spot the one that's stuck or done even when tabs overflow.
- **Open as many sessions as you want** – the tab cap is gone; run sessions browser-style with no limit.
- **Update right from the header** – the “update available” pill is now a one-click shortcut into Settings → Community plugins.
- **Reload a stuck session in place** – a new header reload control offers a soft or hard reload without restarting Obsidian.

## Bug fixes

- **Restored tabs keep their history** – reopening Obsidian no longer leaves a restored tab blank when its local session file is missing (#62).
- **Renamed tabs remember their name** – a tab you renamed before it reconnected now keeps that name across restarts (#70).
- **New chat works from a cold start** – starting a chat with no panel open now opens one instead of silently doing nothing (#74).
- **Notifications focus the right window** – clicking a completion notification now foregrounds the vault window it came from (#60).
- **Auto-detect finds version-managed agents** – agents installed via nvm/fnm/asdf and similar now resolve and launch correctly (#68, #71).
- **Pinned-note tracker keeps up with renames** – renaming your active note no longer shows a stale name on the pin (#58).
- **Hardened macOS agent path resolution** – better fallback to known install locations when a shell lookup fails (synced from upstream).
- **Context pill polish** – the remove (×) button keeps its size next to long note names (#76).

## Removed commands – action needed

To match the simpler command set, three commands were removed: **Open new chat view**, **New session tab**, and the per-agent **Switch agent to [Agent]** commands. If you had hotkeys bound to any of them, rebind under **Settings → Hotkeys** to **New chat** / **New chat with agent…**.

## Internal changes

- Standardized all console output under a single `[Agent Console]` prefix (#78).

## Contributors

Thanks to the upstream RAIT-09 project for the macOS agent path-resolution fixes pulled in via sync.

## Install

**Settings → Community plugins → Browse → “Agent Console” → Install → Enable.** Existing users update via Settings → Community plugins → Check for updates.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Agent Console v1.1.5 blends into your theme and smooths out a batch of reliability rough edges.

## What's new

- **Looks native in any theme** – the chat now draws from Obsidian's own theme variables, so it blends into your light or dark theme (and community themes) instead of carrying its own colors.
- **Clearer context-pin wording** – the context strip uses consistent, plain language for pinning notes to a conversation, so it's obvious what's attached to the current chat.

## Bug fixes

- **Switching agents on a fresh tab works** – pick a different agent on a new tab before sending, and your first message now goes to the agent you chose. (#43)
- **No more stuck "Connecting…" header** – agents that don't report a model name no longer leave the header frozen on "Connecting…". (#41)
- **Links open where you expect** – internal `[[note]]` links in chat now follow Obsidian's own click rules, so Cmd/Ctrl-click and middle-click open in a new pane as they do everywhere else. (#34)
- **Cleaner reloads** – the plugin now closes any open menus when it unloads, so updating it through BRAT or toggling it off no longer leaves stray popups behind.
- **Backspace clears the auto-context pill** – on a new session, Backspace removes the provisional context pill as expected. (#33)
- **Accurate diff highlighting** – word-level highlighting now renders correctly for edits that don't end in a newline. (#36)

## Internal changes

- Reproducible docs-site screenshot tooling plus a static consistency check that runs on every PR. (#47)
- Perf-regression gate (warn-only) and a token-efficiency benchmark added to CI. (#38, #39)
- PR-time tripwire that blocks non-public agent ids from test fixtures. (#45)
- Build and type-check fixes for the benchmark tooling. (#46)
- Docs site accuracy and README polish. (#30, #31, #40, #42)

## Install

In Obsidian, go to **Settings → Community plugins → Browse**, search for **Agent Console**, then Install and Enable.

releases.author: donivatamazondotcom
releases.description:
Clear Obsidian Community Plugins automated-review errors (lint plugin 0.3.0):
- scroll-behavior writes assign from a variable (the rule's dynamic-value escape) instead of setCssProps with a literal key
- vitest setup uses window instead of globalThis (no-global-this)
- pin eslint-plugin-obsidianmd to 0.3.0 so local lint matches the review bot

No runtime behavior change; 327 tests passing.

- Remove unused eslint-disable in ChatPanel
- Fix test suite (activeDocument/setCssProps jsdom shims); 327 tests passing

Fix Community Plugins automated review findings:
- Resolve all eslint-disable errors (specify rules, add descriptions)
- Use window.setTimeout/clearTimeout/requestAnimationFrame for popout window compatibility
- Use activeDocument instead of bare document for popout window compatibility

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
**Agent Console v1.1.0 — the release where the fork finally feels like the fork from message one.**

This is the mental-model release. Four changes shift how Agent Console works at its core: your chats own their context, tabs survive restarts, sessions wake up only when you need them, and there's one clear home for the panel.

## What's new

- **Context belongs to the chat, not the cursor** – when you bring a note into a conversation, that conversation keeps it. Switching notes in your editor no longer silently changes what the agent is looking at. You decide what's in context, and it stays put until you change it.
- **Restart Obsidian without losing your place** – your open tabs, their conversations, and scroll position reopen exactly as you left them. Each sidebar pane restores its own tabs independently.
- **Tabs wake up when you start typing** – opening a tab no longer starts an agent session right away. The session connects the moment you type, so you can reread an old conversation without spinning up an agent.
- **One clear home for chat** – Agent Console now lives in the sidebar, dockable left or right. The floating window and editor-area placements are gone, leaving a single consistent model.
- **Kiro CLI built in** – a fourth built-in agent alongside Claude Code, Codex, and Gemini CLI. Signs in with your Kiro account (no API key); command `kiro-cli acp`.
- **Broadcast to every tab at once** – new commands send the same prompt to all open tabs together, so you can fan a question across parallel agents.

## Heads-up for upgraders

- **Your sessions move folders automatically** – v1.0.x wrote session files into the upstream `agent-client` folder by mistake. v1.1.0 uses its own `agent-console` folder and runs a one-time copy on first load (newer copy wins on collision, never clobbers a live session). Nothing for you to do.
- **Floating chat is gone** – chat lives in the sidebar now. If you opened it in the editor area or a split, it now opens in the right sidebar; legacy placement settings are coerced automatically.
- **"Toggle auto-mention" is now "Toggle active note in context"** – same hotkey (the command id is unchanged); it now grabs/ungrabs the active note in the context strip.
- **Two settings removed** – Max note length and Max selection length are now a fixed 10,000-character limit. Long mentions and selections still truncate but keep a link back to the full note.

## Bug fixes

- **Tabs reliably restore their sessions after a restart** – fixes a family of issues where restored tabs lost their session, showed blank content, or dropped their saved title.
- **Image paste works on fresh and restored tabs** – screenshot paste is no longer blocked before a tab's first keystroke or after a restart.
- **Messages sent while a session is still connecting no longer get stuck** – queued sends dispatch cleanly once the session is ready, with any attached image.
- **Crystallized context survives a restart** – notes pinned to a chat persist with the session instead of being dropped on save.
- **Smoother, quieter UI** – no more double tooltips on the context strip and agent label, and the context strip no longer jumps in height as you add or remove pills.

## Internal changes

- Migrated session storage from the legacy `agent-client` directory to `agent-console` (one-time, newer-wins, copies-never-deletes).
- Dropped the floating-chat component tree and the editor/split placement code paths.
- Synced three fixes from upstream `RAIT-09/obsidian-agent-client`: WSL path normalization on session restore, Windows-only case-insensitive path comparison, and surfacing real ACP error detail instead of `[object Object]`.

## Install

Via [BRAT](https://github.com/TfTHacker/obsidian42-brat): `donivatamazondotcom/obsidian-agent-console`

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Faster, smoother streaming and session restore – plus a fresh look. The chat scroll behavior is rebuilt on a more stable foundation, sessions reopen ~7× faster, and the header and ribbon get a redesign.

## What's new

- **Smooth auto-scroll while agents stream** – the chat stays pinned to the latest content during code blocks, tool calls, multi-tab streaming, and tab activation on long sessions. No more jumping or losing your place mid-response.
- **Sessions reopen 7× faster** – cold-start session restore went from ~5,750 ms to ~809 ms on a 201-message / 56-code-block session. Heavy sessions feel responsive again instead of locking up the UI for several seconds.
- **At-a-glance header** – the chat header now shows the plugin name, active profile, and model so you always know which agent and configuration the current tab is using. (#13)
- **Refreshed ribbon icon** – the Agent Console ribbon icon is now a custom mark with a horizontal-dash eye, matching the plugin's identity across the ribbon, tab bar, and floating button. (#9, #12)
- **Tool calls collapse to one-row summaries** – tool calls take up less screen real estate by default. Click any row to expand and see the full arguments and output when you need them. (#10)

## Bug fixes

- **Stable scroll behavior** – fixes the auto-scroll pin failures users hit on v1.0.1: sub-threshold oscillation during streaming, multi-tab streaming pin loss, and sustained jitter on tab activation. Closes I-S6 (session restore landed at ~3/4 down), I-S7 (~1/4 down after the first fix), I-S9 (~31 ms forced layout on tab activation), I-S10 (synchronous mass-mount cost on heavy tabs), and I-S12 (pin dropped when typing multi-line prompts). (#14)
- **No more streaming-time scroll jitter** – fixes the dual scroll-authority issue where the viewport jumped during streaming. (#6, superseded by #14)

## Internal changes

- Dropped `@tanstack/react-virtual` dependency in favor of native browser scroll.
- Hardcoded Obsidian formatting hints; removed prompt-injection toggle settings. (#11)
- Added Vitest + JSDOM unit-test infrastructure with 56 tests covering pin-state transitions, content-grow auto-anchoring, scroll-event self-echo gating, and post-commit-effect mechanism guards.

## Known issues

Three Low-severity issues are deferred to a future release; none block normal usage:

- **I-S8** – TabBar `requestAnimationFrame` scroll-into-view forces ~73 ms layout on fast tab switching. Not user-perceptible.
- **I-S11** – InputArea focus restoration on cross-pane tab activation triggers brief forced-reflow violations in devtools console. Focus lands correctly; ~30 ms post-click work imperceptible on modern hardware.
- **I-S13** – Auto-scroll pin drops when the prompt textarea reaches its `max-height` (~13 newlines in default config). Workaround: click the scroll-to-bottom pill after the textarea hits its limit.

## Install

Via [BRAT](https://github.com/TfTHacker/obsidian42-brat): `donivatamazondotcom/obsidian-agent-console`

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## What's new

- **Context note badge always renders** – the badge stays visible in the chat input even when no note is opened, and stays in sync with the auto-mention setting. (from upstream #253)

## Bug fixes

- **Hotkeys now route to whichever panel you last interacted with** – ACP commands like new-chat, cancel, switch-agent, and toggle-auto-mention work correctly even when both sidebar tabs and a floating chat are open. (#7, #8)
- **Tabs scroll into view on hotkey switch** – with many tabs open, hotkey-driven tab switches now scroll the active tab into view instead of leaving it offscreen. (#5)
- **Plugin no longer creates dynamic script elements** – addresses an Obsidian Community Plugins review concern by downgrading React from 19 to 18. No user-visible behavior change. (#4)
- **Slash commands no longer pull in stale context** – auto-mention prefix and resource blocks are now skipped on slash-command turns. (from upstream #255, #256)
- **Mode and model restore correctly on session resume** – fixes a race that could leave a resumed session with defaults instead of saved values. (from upstream #281)

## Install

Via [BRAT](https://github.com/TfTHacker/obsidian42-brat): `donivatamazondotcom/obsidian-agent-console`

BRAT users with auto-update enabled will pick up v1.0.1 within ~24h. Otherwise, BRAT settings → "Check for updates".
releases.name: v1.0.1

releases.author: donivatamazondotcom
releases.description:
First release of Agent Console, a fork of [RAIT-09/obsidian-agent-client](https://github.com/RAIT-09/obsidian-agent-client) that adds tabbed multi-session support and ships under a separate plugin identity.

## What's in 1.0.0

- Tabbed multi-session UX in the right sidebar
- Multi-agent support: Kiro, Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI, custom ACP-compatible agents
- Bug fixes carried over from upstream contributions (#281, #282, #283 and earlier)

## Install via BRAT

1. Install [BRAT](https://github.com/TfTHacker/obsidian42-brat)
2. "Add Beta Plugin" → paste `donivatamazondotcom/obsidian-agent-console`
3. Enable Agent Console under Community Plugins

## License

Apache-2.0, inherited from upstream. See [NOTICE](https://github.com/donivatamazondotcom/obsidian-agent-console/blob/main/NOTICE) for attribution.
releases.name: v1.0.0 — Initial release of the Agent Console fork
